metadata.archetype_justification: No database connections or persistent state detected. The primary function is stateless code generation from OpenAPI specifications via CLI or REST endpoint. All operations are read-only transformations (spec in, code out). Classified as stateless-utility.
metadata.context: Code-generation toolkit that produces clients/servers from OpenAPI specs.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — Docker images exist but no container orchestration (ECS/EKS/Fargate) or serverless deployment
top_gaps.impact: Cannot auto-scale, no managed health checks, manual deployment model

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration found
top_gaps.impact: No traceability for production operations or security forensics

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.gap: No identity provider integration; online service has no authentication
top_gaps.impact: Open API surface with no access control

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No VPC, security group, or network segmentation configuration

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.impact: Cannot trace requests through the online service or diagnose latency issues

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (extensive docs/, website/, README.md, per-generator documentation in docs/generators/ with 100+ files)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A RAG-based knowledge agent that indexes project documentation to answer developer questions about generator usage, customization, and template authoring
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index docs/ directory and website/ content into a vector store (e.g., Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases)

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3; 105 GitHub Actions workflows covering build, test, and release)
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A DevOps agent that triggers sample regeneration, checks build status across workflows, and manages releases to Maven Central and Docker Hub
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API for agent interaction; define agent permissions for workflow dispatch

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 4 (already well-decomposed modular architecture); primary trigger not met

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2 = 4 meets threshold; pathway not needed

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Dockerfiles exist (container definitions found); contextual guard blocks: workload already containerized

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1 < 3 but contextual guard blocks: Dockerfiles and multi-stage builds already present

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload is already containerized (Dockerfile, .hub.cli.dockerfile, .hub.online.dockerfile with multi-stage builds). The Move to Containers pathway targets EC2/VM workloads with no containerization.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected; no proprietary SQL. DATA-Q4 is N/A (no database).

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No persistent data store; INF-Q2 is Not Evaluated (archetype-N/A, has_persistent_data_store=false)

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads detected; stateless-utility archetype with no streaming/ETL artifacts. Contextual guard: no evidence of data processing responsibilities.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10 = 2 (partial IaC — no infrastructure definitions for deployment environment)

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q10 < 3: No IaC defines the deployment target environment

detail.current_state: Extensive CI/CD (105 workflows) with build/test/publish but no deployment automation. Dockerfiles exist but no IaC for deployment targets.
immediate_actions: Define IaC for online service deployment (EKS or ECS Fargate)
Add deployment automation stage to CI/CD pipeline
Implement blue/green or canary deployment for the online service

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context mentions 'code-generation' and 'api' but none of the 17 AI-related signal terms.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.

findings.title: No managed compute orchestration
findings.description: All compute is defined only as Docker images without any managed container orchestration or serverless deployment. Images are published to Docker Hub but no ECS, EKS, Fargate, Lambda, or App Runner resources are defined.
findings.gap: No managed compute adoption. Docker images exist but are deployed manually with no orchestration, auto-scaling, or health management.
findings.recommendation: Deploy the online service to EKS (per preferences) or ECS Fargate with managed health checks, auto-scaling, and service discovery. Define the deployment as IaC.

findings.title: No network security configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, NACL, or network segmentation configuration found. The online service exposes port 8080 via Docker with no network isolation defined.
findings.gap: Services would be deployed without network isolation — no private subnets, no security groups, no network segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets for the online service. Deploy behind an ALB or API Gateway in public subnets with the application in private subnets.

findings.title: No managed API entry point
findings.description: The online service runs on port 8080 with embedded Tomcat. No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront configuration exists.
findings.gap: No managed API entry point — no throttling, authentication, request validation, or traffic management at the gateway level.
findings.recommendation: Place the online service behind API Gateway with throttling, authentication, and request validation.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configured
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration found. The online service runs as a single Docker container with no scaling mechanisms.
findings.gap: Service cannot respond to traffic spikes. A single container handles all load with no horizontal scaling.
findings.recommendation: Deploy to EKS with HPA or ECS with target tracking scaling policies. Code generation is CPU-intensive, so CPU-based scaling is appropriate.

findings.title: Minimal IaC coverage
findings.description: Dockerfiles define container builds (partial IaC). No infrastructure definitions exist for deploying those containers — no compute provisioning, networking, load balancing, monitoring, or operational resources are defined.
findings.gap: 0% of deployment infrastructure is defined in IaC. Only container build definitions exist.
findings.recommendation: Define the full deployment stack in IaC (CDK or Terraform): VPC, EKS cluster, service definitions, load balancer, auto-scaling, monitoring.

findings.title: CI/CD lacks deployment automation
findings.description: 105 GitHub Actions workflows covering build, test, and artifact publishing. No deployment automation exists because no target infrastructure is defined.
findings.gap: Pipeline ends at artifact publishing (Docker Hub/Maven Central). No automated deployment to running infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Extend the release pipeline with a deployment stage that deploys the online service Docker image to EKS/ECS.

findings.title: Compound legacy: Java 11 + Spring Boot 2.5 + Springfox
findings.description: Java 11 compile target with Spring Boot 2.5.14 (EOL) and Springfox 3.0.0 (unmaintained). Runtime uses JDK 17.
findings.gap: Compound regression across language version AND framework. Spring Boot 2.5.x is EOL (no security patches). Springfox is abandoned.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to Java 17 compile target, Spring Boot 3.x, and replace Springfox with SpringDoc OpenAPI.

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: The online service has no API versioning. No /v1/ URL patterns, version headers, or versioning annotations.
findings.gap: No API versioning on the online service. Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-path versioning (e.g., /v1/generate) for the online service API.

findings.title: No service discovery mechanism
findings.description: Standalone Spring Boot application with fixed port 8080. No service discovery, service mesh, or dynamic endpoint resolution.
findings.gap: No service discovery mechanism. Endpoint is statically configured.
findings.recommendation: When deploying to EKS, leverage Kubernetes service discovery. If using ECS, configure AWS Cloud Map.

findings.title: Templates and outputs on local filesystem
findings.description: Templates stored within JAR resources. Generated output written to local filesystem (CLI) or returned via HTTP (online). No managed object storage.
findings.gap: No managed object storage for persisting generated code, caching generations, or storing custom templates.
findings.recommendation: Use S3 for persisting generated code outputs with expiring presigned URLs for download in the online service.

findings.title: Mostly centralized data access with minor gaps
findings.description: Well-structured internal data access through TemplateManager, DefaultGenerator, and CodegenConfig interfaces. Swagger Parser provides centralized spec reading.
findings.gap: Minor — some generators access template resources directly rather than through centralized layer.
findings.recommendation: No significant action needed. Current design is appropriate.

findings.title: No audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail, audit logging, or structured request logging. SLF4J provides basic console logging only.
findings.gap: No audit logging for the online service. API calls not traceable. No forensic analysis capability.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail. Add structured request logging (JSON) with correlation IDs. Ship logs to CloudWatch Logs with retention policy.

findings.title: No API authentication
findings.description: Online service exposes code generation API with no authentication. No Spring Security, no API keys, no OAuth2/JWT.
findings.gap: API endpoints are open with no authentication mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Add API authentication via API Gateway with Cognito authorizer or JWT validation. For internal deployment, IAM-based auth via API Gateway.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No identity provider integration. The online service has no authentication and manages no user identities.
findings.gap: No centralized identity integration. The service is completely open.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Cognito or corporate IdP via OIDC when deploying to production.

findings.title: No runtime secrets management
findings.description: CI secrets managed via GitHub Actions secrets (no plaintext). No runtime secrets management (Secrets Manager, Vault) configured for the application.
findings.gap: No runtime secrets management system for AWS deployment.
findings.recommendation: Use Secrets Manager or SSM Parameter Store (SecureString) for runtime configuration with sensitive values.

findings.title: No container image vulnerability scanning
findings.description: Docker images use eclipse-temurin:17-jre (well-maintained). Multi-stage builds minimize attack surface. No vulnerability scanning configured.
findings.gap: No container image vulnerability scanning. No automated patching or rebuild triggers.
findings.recommendation: Add container image scanning in CI (ECR scanning, Trivy, or Snyk). Configure Dependabot for base image updates.

findings.title: No dependency vulnerability scanning in pipeline
findings.description: SpotBugs, PMD, Checkstyle, and forbiddenapis configured for code quality but not security. No Dependabot, OWASP dependency-check, or Snyk.
findings.gap: No dependency vulnerability scanning. No SAST focused on security. No container scanning. No blocking security gate.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dependabot. Add OWASP dependency-check or Snyk. Add ECR image scanning. Configure pipeline gate for critical vulnerabilities.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or tracing library in dependencies. Only SLF4J for basic logging.
findings.gap: No tracing capability. Cannot trace requests or correlate logs.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry Java agent to the online service Docker image. Configure X-Ray as tracing backend.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: No custom metrics published. No generation success/failure rates, language usage, or latency tracking.
findings.gap: Cannot measure generation throughput, success rates, or usage patterns.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch custom metrics for generation requests, failures, latency, and spec size distribution.

findings.title: No alerting configured
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, anomaly detection, or notification integration.
findings.gap: Service degradation or failures would go unnoticed.
findings.recommendation: Define CloudWatch alarms for error rate, latency p99, CPU. Enable anomaly detection. Configure SNS notifications.

findings.title: Deployment strategy limited to Docker Hub push
findings.description: Docker images published to Docker Hub via automated workflow. No canary or blue/green deployment because no deployment target exists.
findings.gap: No deployment strategy beyond Docker Hub push. No staged rollout.
findings.recommendation: Implement rolling updates with readiness probes on EKS. Consider ArgoCD for GitOps-based deployment.

findings.title: Integration tests focused on generated code, not online service API
findings.description: CircleCI integration tests validate generated code against Petstore mock. 90+ sample workflows test generated code. No dedicated online service API test suite.
findings.gap: No integration tests for the online service's own HTTP API behavior.
findings.recommendation: Add integration tests exercising the online service API: varying spec sizes, malformed specs, concurrent requests.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, incident automation, self-healing patterns, or SSM Automation documents.
findings.gap: Incident response entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common scenarios. Store as SSM Automation documents.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no per-service dashboards, no team-attributed alarms.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — monitoring infrastructure is nonexistent.
findings.recommendation: Define observability ownership in CODEOWNERS. Create service dashboard. Assign alarm ownership.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resource tagging — no IaC exists to tag. No tagging standard defined.
findings.gap: No tagging governance for cost allocation, ownership, or environment identification.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter). Implement via default_tags in Terraform or CDK aspects.

evaluations.reason: stateless-utility archetype; no multi-step workflows exist
stateless-utility archetype; synchronous HTTP is the correct design
No persistent state to back up
Well-decomposed modular architecture with 7 independent modules
stateless-utility archetype; sync is correct design
stateless-utility archetype; no operations exceed 30 seconds
No deployed monitoring infrastructure to evaluate SLOs against
